summary.intent: Address all 5 Codex automated review findings on the color picker component in a Rust UI library, using TDD, and sync the spec documentation to match.
summary.outcome: All 5 fixes implemented, 85 tests passing, clippy clean, branch coverage held at 90.1%, snapshots updated, spec synced, and changes committed and pushed.

repo: Color space changes must route through the ChangeColorSpace event rather than the blanket SetProps sync; otherwise an unrelated prop update silently reverts a runtime color space switch.
The disabled guard should block user-initiated Toggle/interaction but must pass through programmatic Open/Close commands.
Close/Toggle/CloseOnEscape must be handled from the Dragging state (abandoning the drag), while interact-outside stays suppressed during pointer capture.
The project uses cargo xcov for branch coverage gating with a threshold (~90% for ars-components); it runs as a background task.

Spec files (spec/components/specialized/color-picker.md) must be manually synced across multiple sections (§1.7 machine block, §1.8 highlight, §2 anatomy table, §3.1 ARIA table) after any contract-level code change.
The project uses the insta crate for snapshot testing; intentional rendering changes require explicit snapshot re-acceptance.
Codex is used as an automated review bot (@codex review) that opens PR threads; the workflow loops commit→push→reply+resolve→re-trigger until a thumbs-up verdict.

code.finding: color_space was incorrectly included in the blanket SetProps sync and context_relevant_props_changed; it must only be driven through ChangeColorSpace to avoid silent runtime value reversal.
Disabled channel thumbs and swatches were missing tabindex="-1" and aria-disabled, unlike the area thumb which already had them.
Trigger button lacked type="button", risking accidental form submission when used inside a <form>.
exhaustive_events_and_parts_walk test asserted disabled kept the picker closed on Open; this was wrong under the corrected semantics and had to be updated.

workflow: TDD discipline maintained: new tests written alongside each fix, not after.
Background tasks (coverage gate, polling loop) are handled via ScheduleWakeup rather than blocking the conversation.
Snapshot diffs should be inspected before accepting to confirm only the intentional change is present.
Spec table realignment is a separate mechanical step after edits to avoid formatting drift.
Codex review threads are replied-to and resolved individually after the push, then @codex review is re-triggered to close the loop.

friction: Coverage gate runs asynchronously, requiring a ScheduleWakeup and an extra round-trip before the commit could proceed.
Multiple disjoint spec sections (§1.7, §1.8, §2, §3.1) required separate reads and edits to stay in sync with code changes — no single canonical location.
Two existing disabled-semantics tests had incorrect expectations that needed updating after the behavioral fix, adding noise to the change.
Three trigger snapshots had to be re-accepted after adding type="button"; required verifying the diff contained only that one addition before accepting.
open_items: Re-trigger @codex review after the push and loop through any new findings until a thumbs-up verdict is received.

Interact-outside suppression during pointer capture (Dragging state) was mentioned as intentionally kept — worth a follow-up review to ensure it behaves correctly across all pointer-capture edge cases.
